evals.name: plan-from-canonical-spec-set
evals.prompt: We have a canonical spec set for the journal app at .specs/journal/specs/ (overview, domain model with Entry, app shell, editor + calendar runtime, persistence + export, and a per-app auth page covering a single-user passphrase lock). The repo's development guidelines are at .specs/development-guidelines.md. Plan the implementation — break the spec into a dependency-ordered graph of reviewable task packages and write the plan to .specs/plans/.
evals.expected_output: A plan folder at .specs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-implement_journal_app/ (ISO date prefix, snake_case title) with plan.md at its root and a kanban board of subfolders — every task authored as NN-snake_case_task.md into backlog/ beside its co-located NN-snake_case_task-certificate.md, numbered in implementation order; in-progress/, blocked/, and done/ are not pre-created (spec-builder makes them as tasks move). plan.md has Status: Draft, Layout: kanban, Date, Owner, Source spec: .specs/journal/specs/; a 'Source and definition-of-done baseline' section pointing the per-task DoD at .specs/development-guidelines.md §Definition of done; a Task graph with BOTH a Mermaid graph TD block AND a dependency table (table marked as source of truth, each row keying a task by number and title — no path link, the file found by glob across the subfolders — listing depends-on and edge kind, with every depends-on referencing a lower number); an Implementation order + milestones section; and the closing Assumptions/Decisions/Open questions block. Each task file is hybrid (header with a Plan link to ../plan.md and a Certificate link, no per-task Status field — the subfolder is the status — then Implements/Depends on/Produces/Pointers, a Steps checklist, and a Definition of done checklist ending in a 'Reviewable:' line). The passphrase lock is numbered 01 and built first because later features are reviewed through it (review edges). Every spec section maps to a task file; .specs/README.md gains a Plans section pointing at plan.md. Spec-page links resolve from the task's subfolder (../../../). No spec prose restated, no MVP framing, no emoji.

evals.name: plan-from-change-spec
evals.prompt: Turn the change spec at .specs/changes/2026-05-21-add_tagging_to_entries.md into an implementation plan. It proposes a Tag entity, a tags object store, and a tag picker in the editor toolbar for the editor app at .specs/editor/specs/. Expand its Implementation notes into a sequenced, reviewable task graph.
evals.expected_output: A plan folder at .specs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-implement_tagging/ whose plan.md (Layout: kanban) Source spec is the change spec. The change spec's Proposed changes and Implementation notes are expanded into separate task files authored into backlog/ (e.g. backlog/01-tag_type_and_parser.md, 02-tags_store_and_migration.md, 03-tag_picker_ui.md, 04-filter_by_tag.md), each beside its NN-…-certificate.md — not merely copied. plan.md's Mermaid + dependency table (keyed by task number, no path links) shows the store/schema task as a data dependency of the UI tasks, with depends-on referencing lower numbers. Each task file names the change-spec section or canonical page it implements and carries a Definition of done tied to the repo guidelines plus a Reviewable line, and has no per-task Status field (its backlog/ subfolder is its status). The store/migration task is numbered before the UI that reads it. .specs/README.md indexes the plan under Plans, pointed at plan.md. If the change is small enough that the change spec's own Implementation notes already suffice, the skill says so rather than padding a folder.

evals.name: plan-from-external-spec-with-auth-ordering
evals.prompt: We have a PRD (not a spec-creator spec) for a small SaaS dashboard: users sign up and log in, then see a billing page, a team-settings page, and a usage-analytics page — all of which require being logged in. There's no development-guidelines page yet. Plan the build and write it to .specs/plans/, making sure the order produces reviewable work early.
evals.expected_output: Because there is no development-guidelines page, the skill establishes the definition-of-done baseline from repo signals or by asking the user, and records that source in plan.md's baseline section. A plan folder at .specs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<snake_case>/ — plan.md (Layout: kanban) at the root with every task authored into backlog/ beside its certificate — with auth (signup + login) as task 01, carrying outgoing REVIEW edges to billing, team-settings, and usage-analytics (none can be reviewed end to end without login). plan.md's Mermaid graph and dependency table (keyed by task number) agree and form a DAG. Each gated page is its own task file ordered after auth, with no per-task Status field (the subfolder is the status). Milestones end at reviewable states (M1: a user can sign up and log in to an empty shell; later milestones add the gated pages). The external source is noted as having no canonical schema, with any type ambiguity captured in plan.md's Open questions. Standard header and closing block; .specs/README.md indexed, pointed at plan.md.
